title: abutilon crispifolium
intro: Abutilon crispifolium, also known as the hairy gourd or hairy abutilon, is a plant species in the mallow family (Malvaceae) that is native to tropical regions. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, and is valued for its ability to treat various health conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin irritations. The health benefits of Abutilon crispifolium include its potential to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing, as well as its ability to act as an antiseptic and astringent. Abutilon crispifolium can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being used in topical applications such as creams and ointments.
